team.name: Emily Carter
team.bio: 10+ years designing covers across fantasy, romance and thriller. Emily blends classical painting techniques with sharp typographic instinct, leading our cover team and mentoring junior artists.
team.process: Starts every cover with three days of genre research and comparative analysis, sketches in greyscale to lock composition, then renders in full color over multiple passes.

reviews.author: Sarah Mitchell
reviews.text: The cover Emily designed for my debut novel doubled my preorders. Worth every penny — and the process was a joy.

reviews.author: Olivia Martins
reviews.text: After three failed attempts with AI-only studios, Emily delivered a real, painterly cover that finally felt like my book.

reviews.author: James O'Connor
reviews.text: Premium agency feel without the agency price. My self-pub looks like a Big Five release.

faqs.q: How fast can Emily start my cover?
faqs.a: Typical lead time is 2–4 weeks. Rush slots open monthly.
Yes — she specializes in series identity across 3-7 book runs.

team.ctaDescription: Custom quotes, transparent process, hand-drawn work. Start your project in minutes.

team.name: Luna Vasquez
team.bio: Cartographer and illustrator with a love for world-building. From sprawling continents to medieval city plans, every line is hand-drawn. Luna leads our maps department and has shipped maps for over 120 published titles.
team.process: Begins with a world-building call to understand geography, politics and history. Drafts in pencil, refines in ink, finishes with watercolor or digital parchment textures.

reviews.author: Daniel Park
reviews.text: Luna built a kingdom-scale map for my trilogy. Readers won't stop sharing it.

reviews.author: Adewale Okafor
reviews.text: The map alone made my book pitch land — agents asked about it before the manuscript.

faqs.q: Can Luna design a map without a finished manuscript?
faqs.a: Yes — a strong outline and world bible is enough to begin.
Standard 300DPI print, high-res poster, and web-optimized exports.

team.name: Isabella Rossi
team.bio: Painterly digital illustrator focused on hero portraits and concept work. Isabella brings characters off the page with strong anatomy, cinematic lighting and an unmistakable signature style.
team.process: Studies references obsessively, runs silhouette thumbnails for the strongest shape, then layers paint over greyscale value studies.

reviews.author: Priya Nair
reviews.text: Isabella's character portraits are the heart of my IP pitch deck. Investors keep asking who painted them.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Tanaka
reviews.text: Three character sheets, three perfect deliveries. Now my permanent character artist.

faqs.q: Can she match an existing character style?
faqs.a: Yes — share reference and she'll match or evolve the look.

team.name: Noah Bennett
team.bio: Identity systems, packaging and digital design. Noah builds brands that feel inevitable. Previously in-house at two agencies before joining The Arts Folio.
team.process: Discovery interviews, competitive audit, three radical directions, one refined system, full guideline document.

reviews.author: Marco Rinaldi
reviews.text: Noah rebuilt our brand from the logo up. Cleaner, sharper, instantly more premium.

reviews.author: Sophie Laurent
reviews.text: Three rounds, one perfect identity. Couldn't have asked for a better partner.

faqs.q: Does Noah build the website himself?
faqs.a: He leads design and partners with our front-end engineers for the build.
Four to eight weeks from kickoff to full guideline document.

team.name: Sophia Chen
team.bio: Award-winning interior illustrator with a warm, painterly style. Sophia has illustrated more than 40 published children's titles and chapter books for major and indie publishers.
team.process: Reads the manuscript twice, sketches every spread in thumbnails, builds a tight pencil pass, then watercolor and digital finishing.

reviews.author: Amelia Schneider
reviews.text: Sophia's interiors made my book a gift-table favorite. Every page is a print.

reviews.author: Yuki Tanaka
reviews.text: Patient, talented, on time. The kind of illustrator publishers fight to book.

faqs.q: Does Sophia work in color and B/W?
faqs.a: Both — picked based on print budget and genre.

faqs.q: Can she illustrate a full picture book?
faqs.a: Yes — full 32-page picture books are her specialty.

interactiveGallery.description: We map out compositions and values early to ensure the storytelling lands before adding complex details.
Clean anatomical line work establishes strong form and silhouette before the painting and rendering phase.
Topography, mountain placement, and kingdom borders are drafted in pencil for approval before final inking.
Loose painterly color sketches lock the lighting scheme and mood before finalizing character details.

processVideos.title: Sketch Timelapses
processVideos.desc: Watch initial ideas materialize on a digital canvas.

processVideos.title: Line Art Process
processVideos.desc: Pencil outlines are transformed into clean, stylized vectors.

processVideos.title: Rendering Process
processVideos.desc: Cinematic shading, highlights, and custom lighting layers are added.

processVideos.title: Character Creation
processVideos.desc: Creating unique silhouettes and expressive features from scratch.

processVideos.title: Fantasy Map Development
processVideos.desc: Painstakingly detailing topography and custom labeling.

processVideos.title: Book Cover Creation
processVideos.desc: Blending character rendering with typography and effects.

watchArtworkSection.title: Watch real artwork being created Live
watchArtworkSection.description: Unlike instant AI-generated images, professional artwork is built through creativity, skill, feedback, and refinement. Our artists document parts of the creative journey, allowing clients to see how concepts evolve into finished pieces.

realCraftsmanshipSection.title: The Real Craftsmanship
realCraftsmanshipSection.description1: Every masterpiece begins with a sketch. Our process videos allow clients to see the real craftsmanship behind each project, creating confidence that their artwork is being professionally developed by experienced artists.
realCraftsmanshipSection.description2: See the difference between generated images and a professionally guided creative process.

startYourProjectCard.title: Want to see how your project will be created?
startYourProjectCard.description: Request a consultation and we'll walk you through our entire creative process.

startYourProjectSection.title: Tell us about your vision
startYourProjectSection.description: A bestselling book cover, a fantasy world map, character artwork, branding, or a complete website — share your brief and we'll respond within 24 hours.

contactPage.title: Let's start a conversation
contactPage.description: Share your brief — we typically reply within a few hours during business days.
